solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1571C - Rhyme
Let $$$\mathit{limit}_i$$$ be the maximum value $$$k$$$ such that the $$$i$$$-th pair rhymes regardless of must it or must not. It's easy to calculate $$$\mathit{limit}_i$$$ as the length of the longest equal suffix of $$$s_i$$$ and $$$t_i$$$.
Now, if we know that the $$$i$$$-th pair must rhyme, then any possible $$$k$$$ should not exceed $$$\mathit{limit}_i$$$. But if the $$$i$$$-th pair must not rhyme, then any possible $$$k$$$ should be strictly greater than $$$\mathit{limit}_i$$$.
In other words, all possible $$$k$$$-s form a segment between minimum possible $$$k$$$ and maximum possible $$$k$$$. So, we can calculate minimum possible $$$k$$$ (named $$$l$$$) as $$$\max(\mathit{limit}_i) + 1$$$ among all $$$i$$$ with $$$r_i = 0$$$ and maximum possible $$$k$$$ (named $$$r$$$) as $$$\min(\mathit{limit}_i)$$$ among all $$$i$$$ with $$$r_i = 1$$$.
As a result there will be $$$\max(0, r - l + 1)$$$ possible $$$k$$$ from $$$l$$$ to $$$r$$$.

solutions.title: Solution (adedalic)
solutions.content:
fun main
()
{
repeat
(
readLine
()!!.
toInt
())
{
val n
=
readLine
()!!.
toInt
()
var
l
=
0
var
r
=
4e5
.
toInt
()
for
(
i
in
1.
.
n
)
{
val pairs
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
' '
)
val limit
=
pairs
[
0
].
commonSuffixWith
(
pairs
[
1
]).
length
if
(
pairs
[
2
]
==
"0"
)
l
=
maxOf
(
l
,
limit
+
1
)
else
r
=
minOf
(
r
,
limit
)
}
println
(
maxOf
(
0
,
r
-
l
+
1
))
if
(
r
-
l
+
1
>
0
)
println
((
l
..
r
).
joinToString
(
" "
))
}
}
